summary: Iteration 117 - GalleryPage Component Extraction & WebSocket Earnings Broadcast Verification. All 7 features verified: (1) GalleryPage.js imports UploadPhotoModal from extracted component (line 19), (2) GalleryPage.js reduced from 2488 to 2178 lines (~310 lines saved), (3) UploadPhotoModal extracted component works independently (319 lines, opens correctly with all form fields), (4) WebSocket /ws/earnings/{user_id} endpoint exists in websocket.py (lines 81-109), (5) Gallery purchase triggers broadcast_earnings_update (gallery.py lines 18, 710-722), (6) WebSocket hooks match backend message types (useWebSocket.js lines 119-161), (7) App loads without console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: GalleryPage.js line count: 2178 (reduced from 2488, saving ~310 lines)
UploadPhotoModal.js is a complete standalone component (319 lines) with all upload functionality
WebSocket endpoint /ws/earnings/{user_id} properly defined in websocket.py (lines 81-109)
broadcast_earnings_update imported in gallery.py (line 18) and called after purchase (lines 710-722)
useWebSocket.js hooks properly match backend message types: 'new_condition_report', 'live_status_change', 'earnings_update'
All files pass ESLint and ruff linting with no issues

success_rate.frontend: 100% (GalleryPage loads, UploadPhotoModal opens correctly, all lint clean)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Component extraction verified. GalleryPage uses extracted UploadPhotoModal. WebSocket earnings broadcast is wired up in gallery.py purchase flow. Frontend hooks match backend message types. Line counts: GalleryPage.js 2178 lines, MapPage.js 1851 lines. Total savings from refactoring: ~350 lines extracted to reusable components.
